message:
Fix ScrollToTop hydration mismatch with deterministic initial state

The useState initializers for isVisible and progress used
window.scrollY and document.documentElement.scrollHeight, which
are undefined during SSR. The lazy initializer pattern
(() => typeof window !== "undefined" && ...) produced different
values on server (false/0) vs client (potentially true/non-zero
if the page was scrolled before hydration), triggering React
hydration warnings.

Fix: initialize both states to deterministic values (false and 0)
and let the existing scroll handler update them on the first
client-side scroll event. This matches the server render exactly
and avoids the brief flash of an incorrect scroll position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
